examples: Hematite is an iron oxide that is deep red in color.
It only takes a little hematite, Merrison said, to stain all the dust a reddish hue.
A widely occurring iron oxide ore; a mixture of goethite and hematite and lepidocrocite.
Also, if you ever meet iron-oxide or hematite, buy them a drink, as it's their presence in the soil that gave us such a good preservation.
